instruction: Implement C++ three-way comparison (spaceship operator <=>) for a version class that handles major.minor.patch with pre-release tags, and show how it automatically generates all six comparison operators.
output:
<think>
C++20 introduced the spaceship operator <=>. When you define operator<=>, the compiler auto-generates ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=. The return type determines the comparison category:
- std::strong_ordering: total order, substitutability (a == b means they're identical)
- std::weak_ordering: total order, no substitutability
- std::partial_ordering: some values may be incomparable

For semantic versioning: major.minor.patch is a strong ordering, but pre-release adds complexity. A version with pre-release is less than the same version without. Pre-release components can be numeric or alphanumeric.

I'll use std::strong_ordering for the numeric parts and handle pre-release comparison manually. The spaceship operator must return a consistent ordering type. Since pre-release can have alphanumeric identifiers, I'll still use strong_ordering (strings have total order).

Key: if I define operator<=> as defaulted, the compiler compares members in declaration order. But I need custom logic for pre-release, so I'll implement it manually. I also need operator== separately because custom <=> doesn't auto-generate == (only defaulted <=> does).

Actually in C++20: if <=> is user-declared (not defaulted), == is NOT auto-generated. I need to declare == explicitly. But if I default ==, it compares member-by-member which is correct here.
</think>

```cpp
#include <compare>
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <sstream>
#include <algorithm>

class SemVer {
public:
    int major, minor, patch;
    std::vector<std::string> prerelease;  // empty = release

    SemVer(int maj, int min, int pat, std::vector<std::string> pre = {})
        : major(maj), minor(min), patch(pat), prerelease(std::move(pre)) {}

    // Parse from string "1.2.3-alpha.1"
    static SemVer parse(const std::string& s) {
        SemVer v{0, 0, 0};
        std::istringstream iss(s);
        char dot;
        iss >> v.major >> dot >> v.minor >> dot >> v.patch;
        if (iss.peek() == '-') {
            iss.get(); // consume '-'
            std::string rest;
            std::getline(iss, rest);
            std::istringstream pss(rest);
            std::string token;
            while (std::getline(pss, token, '.')) {
                v.prerelease.push_back(token);
            }
        }
        return v;
    }

    // Three-way comparison: auto-generates <, >, <=, >=
    std::strong_ordering operator<=>(const SemVer& other) const {
        // Compare numeric version first
        if (auto cmp = major <=> other.major; cmp != 0) return cmp;
        if (auto cmp = minor <=> other.minor; cmp != 0) return cmp;
        if (auto cmp = patch <=> other.patch; cmp != 0) return cmp;

        // Pre-release precedence (semver spec):
        // - Release (empty pre) > pre-release
        // - Compare identifiers left to right
        // - Numeric < alpha; numeric compared as ints; alpha as strings
        bool a_has_pre = !prerelease.empty();
        bool b_has_pre = !other.prerelease.empty();

        if (!a_has_pre && !b_has_pre) return std::strong_ordering::equal;
        if (!a_has_pre && b_has_pre) return std::strong_ordering::greater;
        if (a_has_pre && !b_has_pre) return std::strong_ordering::less;

        // Both have pre-release: compare identifier by identifier
        size_t len = std::min(prerelease.size(), other.prerelease.size());
        for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {
            bool a_num = is_numeric(prerelease[i]);
            bool b_num = is_numeric(other.prerelease[i]);

            if (a_num && b_num) {
                int ai = std::stoi(prerelease[i]);
                int bi = std::stoi(other.prerelease[i]);
                if (auto cmp = ai <=> bi; cmp != 0) return cmp;
            } else if (a_num) {
                return std::strong_ordering::less; // numeric < alpha
            } else if (b_num) {
                return std::strong_ordering::greater;
            } else {
                if (auto cmp = prerelease[i] <=> other.prerelease[i]; cmp != 0) return cmp;
            }
        }
        return prerelease.size() <=> other.prerelease.size();
    }

    // Must explicitly define == when <=> is user-provided
    bool operator==(const SemVer& other) const {
        return (*this <=> other) == std::strong_ordering::equal;
    }

    friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const SemVer& v) {
        os << v.major << '.' << v.minor << '.' << v.patch;
        if (!v.prerelease.empty()) {
            os << '-';
            for (size_t i = 0; i < v.prerelease.size(); ++i) {
                if (i > 0) os << '.';
                os << v.prerelease[i];
            }
        }
        return os;
    }

private:
    static bool is_numeric(const std::string& s) {
        return !s.empty() && std::all_of(s.begin(), s.end(), ::isdigit);
    }
};

int main() {
    auto v1 = SemVer::parse("1.0.0-alpha");
    auto v2 = SemVer::parse("1.0.0-alpha.1");
    auto v3 = SemVer::parse("1.0.0-beta");
    auto v4 = SemVer::parse("1.0.0");

    // All six operators auto-generated from <=>
    std::cout << std::boolalpha;
    std::cout << (v1 < v2) << '\n';   // true: alpha < alpha.1
    std::cout << (v2 < v3) << '\n';   // true: alpha.1 < beta
    std::cout << (v3 < v4) << '\n';   // true: pre-release < release
    std::cout << (v4 > v1) << '\n';   // true
    std::cout << (v1 == v1) << '\n';  // true
    std::cout << (v1 != v3) << '\n';  // true

    // Sortable
    std::vector<SemVer> versions = {v4, v1, v3, v2};
    std::sort(versions.begin(), versions.end());
    for (const auto& v : versions) std::cout << v << '\n';
    // 1.0.0-alpha, 1.0.0-alpha.1, 1.0.0-beta, 1.0.0
}
```

instruction: Implement C++ stream insertion and extraction operators (operator<< and operator>>) for a JSON-like value type that handles nested objects, arrays, and pretty-printing with configurable indentation.
output:
<think>
I need to implement operator<< for output (serialization) and operator>> for input (parsing) of a JSON-like type. Key design:

1. JValue variant type: Null, Bool, Number, String, Array, Object
2. operator<< should produce formatted JSON with configurable indentation
3. operator>> should parse JSON from a stream
4. These must be friend functions (non-member) since the left operand is ostream/istream

For pretty-printing, I need to track indentation depth. Since operator<< gets a reference to ostream and JValue, I can't easily pass indentation state. Options:
- Use a manipulator-like wrapper class
- Use iword/pword on the stream for state
- Use a recursive helper method that takes depth

I'll use the cleanest approach: operator<< calls a serialize() method that takes ostream and depth. For compact vs pretty, I'll use stream flags via xalloc.

For operator>>, I'll implement a simple recursive descent parser that reads from the stream.
</think>

```cpp
#include <iostream>
#include <map>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>
#include <variant>
#include <vector>

class JValue;
using JArray = std::vector<JValue>;
using JObject = std::map<std::string, JValue>;

class JValue {
public:
    using Variant = std::variant<std::nullptr_t, bool, double, std::string, JArray, JObject>;
    Variant data;

    JValue() : data(nullptr) {}
    JValue(std::nullptr_t) : data(nullptr) {}
    JValue(bool b) : data(b) {}
    JValue(double d) : data(d) {}
    JValue(int i) : data(static_cast<double>(i)) {}
    JValue(const char* s) : data(std::string(s)) {}
    JValue(std::string s) : data(std::move(s)) {}
    JValue(JArray a) : data(std::move(a)) {}
    JValue(JObject o) : data(std::move(o)) {}

    // Pretty-print control via stream state
    static int indent_index() {
        static int idx = std::ios_base::xalloc();
        return idx;
    }

    void serialize(std::ostream& os, int depth, int indent) const {
        std::string pad(depth * indent, ' ');
        std::string inner((depth + 1) * indent, ' ');
        bool pretty = indent > 0;

        std::visit([&](auto&& val) {
            using T = std::decay_t<decltype(val)>;
            if constexpr (std::is_same_v<T, std::nullptr_t>) {
                os << "null";
            } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<T, bool>) {
                os << (val ? "true" : "false");
            } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<T, double>) {
                if (val == static_cast<int>(val)) os << static_cast<int>(val);
                else os << val;
            } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<T, std::string>) {
                os << '"';
                for (char c : val) {
                    switch (c) {
                        case '"': os << "\\\""; break;
                        case '\\': os << "\\\\"; break;
                        case '\n': os << "\\n"; break;
                        case '\t': os << "\\t"; break;
                        default: os << c;
                    }
                }
                os << '"';
            } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<T, JArray>) {
                os << '[';
                for (size_t i = 0; i < val.size(); ++i) {
                    if (pretty) os << '\n' << inner;
                    val[i].serialize(os, depth + 1, indent);
                    if (i + 1 < val.size()) os << ',';
                }
                if (pretty && !val.empty()) os << '\n' << pad;
                os << ']';
            } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<T, JObject>) {
                os << '{';
                size_t i = 0;
                for (const auto& [key, value] : val) {
                    if (pretty) os << '\n' << inner;
                    os << '"' << key << '"' << ':' << (pretty ? " " : "");
                    value.serialize(os, depth + 1, indent);
                    if (++i < val.size()) os << ',';
                }
                if (pretty && !val.empty()) os << '\n' << pad;
                os << '}';
            }
        }, data);
    }

    // operator<< uses indent from stream iword
    friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const JValue& v) {
        int indent = static_cast<int>(os.iword(indent_index()));
        v.serialize(os, 0, indent);
        return os;
    }

    // operator>> parses JSON from stream
    friend std::istream& operator>>(std::istream& is, JValue& v) {
        v = parse_value(is);
        return is;
    }

private:
    static void skip_ws(std::istream& is) {
        while (is.peek() == ' ' || is.peek() == '\n' ||
               is.peek() == '\r' || is.peek() == '\t') is.get();
    }

    static JValue parse_value(std::istream& is) {
        skip_ws(is);
        char c = is.peek();
        if (c == '"') return parse_string(is);
        if (c == '{') return parse_object(is);
        if (c == '[') return parse_array(is);
        if (c == 't' || c == 'f') return parse_bool(is);
        if (c == 'n') return parse_null(is);
        return parse_number(is);
    }

    static JValue parse_string(std::istream& is) {
        is.get(); // consume "
        std::string s;
        while (is.peek() != '"') {
            char c = is.get();
            if (c == '\\') { char esc = is.get(); s += esc; }
            else s += c;
        }
        is.get(); // consume closing "
        return JValue(s);
    }

    static JValue parse_number(std::istream& is) { double d; is >> d; return JValue(d); }
    static JValue parse_bool(std::istream& is) {
        std::string w; for (int i = 0; i < (is.peek() == 't' ? 4 : 5); ++i) w += is.get();
        return JValue(w == "true");
    }
    static JValue parse_null(std::istream& is) { for (int i=0;i<4;++i) is.get(); return JValue(nullptr); }
    static JValue parse_array(std::istream& is) {
        is.get(); JArray arr; skip_ws(is);
        if (is.peek() != ']') {
            arr.push_back(parse_value(is)); skip_ws(is);
            while (is.peek() == ',') { is.get(); arr.push_back(parse_value(is)); skip_ws(is); }
        }
        is.get(); return JValue(arr);
    }
    static JValue parse_object(std::istream& is) {
        is.get(); JObject obj; skip_ws(is);
        if (is.peek() != '}') {
            auto key = std::get<std::string>(parse_string(is).data);
            skip_ws(is); is.get(); // colon
            obj[key] = parse_value(is); skip_ws(is);
            while (is.peek() == ',') {
                is.get(); skip_ws(is);
                key = std::get<std::string>(parse_string(is).data);
                skip_ws(is); is.get();
                obj[key] = parse_value(is); skip_ws(is);
            }
        }
        is.get(); return JValue(obj);
    }
};

// Pretty-print manipulator
struct pretty { int indent; pretty(int n = 2) : indent(n) {} };
std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const pretty& p) {
    os.iword(JValue::indent_index()) = p.indent;
    return os;
}

int main() {
    JValue config(JObject{
        {"name", "app"},
        {"version", 2},
        {"features", JArray{"auth", "logging", true}},
        {"db", JObject{{"host", "localhost"}, {"port", 5432}}}
    });
    std::cout << pretty(2) << config << '\n';
}
```

instruction: Implement C++ arithmetic operator overloading for a fixed-point number class with configurable precision, overflow detection, and implicit/explicit conversion operators to and from floating-point types.
output:
<think>
Fixed-point arithmetic stores numbers as integers with an implied decimal point. For example, with 8 fractional bits, the value 1.5 is stored as 384 (1.5 * 256). This gives exact decimal arithmetic (no floating-point rounding) and fast integer-based computation.

Design decisions:
1. Template parameter for fractional bits (compile-time precision)
2. Internal storage as int64_t for wide range
3. Arithmetic operators: +, -, *, / with overflow detection
4. Comparison operators via <=> (C++20)
5. Explicit conversion to double/float (explicit to prevent accidental precision loss)
6. Implicit construction from int (safe, no precision loss)
7. Overflow detection: check if result would exceed int64_t range before operation

For multiplication: (a * 2^F) * (b * 2^F) = a*b * 2^(2F), so we need to shift right by F after multiplying. For division: (a * 2^F) / (b * 2^F) = a/b, so we need to shift left by F before dividing.

Overflow risk is highest in multiplication. I'll use __int128 for the intermediate product to avoid overflow.
</think>

```cpp
#include <cstdint>
#include <compare>
#include <iostream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <type_traits>

template <int FracBits = 16>
class Fixed {
    static_assert(FracBits > 0 && FracBits < 62, "FracBits must be in [1, 61]");

    int64_t raw_;
    static constexpr int64_t SCALE = int64_t(1) << FracBits;
    static constexpr int64_t MAX_RAW = INT64_MAX;
    static constexpr int64_t MIN_RAW = INT64_MIN;

    // Private constructor from raw value
    struct RawTag {};
    constexpr Fixed(int64_t raw, RawTag) : raw_(raw) {}

public:
    constexpr Fixed() : raw_(0) {}

    // Implicit from integer (safe, exact)
    constexpr Fixed(int val) : raw_(static_cast<int64_t>(val) * SCALE) {}
    constexpr Fixed(int64_t val) : raw_(val * SCALE) {}

    // Explicit from floating point (lossy)
    explicit constexpr Fixed(double val) : raw_(static_cast<int64_t>(val * SCALE + (val >= 0 ? 0.5 : -0.5))) {}
    explicit constexpr Fixed(float val) : Fixed(static_cast<double>(val)) {}

    // Explicit conversion to floating point
    explicit constexpr operator double() const { return static_cast<double>(raw_) / SCALE; }
    explicit constexpr operator float() const { return static_cast<float>(static_cast<double>(*this)); }
    explicit constexpr operator int() const { return static_cast<int>(raw_ / SCALE); }

    // Raw access
    constexpr int64_t raw() const { return raw_; }
    static constexpr Fixed from_raw(int64_t r) { return Fixed(r, RawTag{}); }

    // --- Arithmetic operators with overflow detection ---

    constexpr Fixed operator+(const Fixed& rhs) const {
        // Overflow check: if both positive and sum negative, or both negative and sum positive
        int64_t result = raw_ + rhs.raw_;
        if ((rhs.raw_ > 0 && raw_ > MAX_RAW - rhs.raw_) ||
            (rhs.raw_ < 0 && raw_ < MIN_RAW - rhs.raw_)) {
            throw std::overflow_error("Fixed-point addition overflow");
        }
        return from_raw(result);
    }

    constexpr Fixed operator-(const Fixed& rhs) const {
        if ((rhs.raw_ < 0 && raw_ > MAX_RAW + rhs.raw_) ||
            (rhs.raw_ > 0 && raw_ < MIN_RAW + rhs.raw_)) {
            throw std::overflow_error("Fixed-point subtraction overflow");
        }
        return from_raw(raw_ - rhs.raw_);
    }

    constexpr Fixed operator*(const Fixed& rhs) const {
        // Use 128-bit intermediate to prevent overflow
        __int128 wide = static_cast<__int128>(raw_) * rhs.raw_;
        __int128 result = wide >> FracBits;  // Remove extra scale factor

        if (result > MAX_RAW || result < MIN_RAW) {
            throw std::overflow_error("Fixed-point multiplication overflow");
        }
        return from_raw(static_cast<int64_t>(result));
    }

    constexpr Fixed operator/(const Fixed& rhs) const {
        if (rhs.raw_ == 0) throw std::domain_error("Fixed-point division by zero");
        // Scale numerator before division to maintain precision
        __int128 wide = static_cast<__int128>(raw_) << FracBits;
        __int128 result = wide / rhs.raw_;

        if (result > MAX_RAW || result < MIN_RAW) {
            throw std::overflow_error("Fixed-point division overflow");
        }
        return from_raw(static_cast<int64_t>(result));
    }

    constexpr Fixed operator-() const { return from_raw(-raw_); }

    // Compound assignment
    constexpr Fixed& operator+=(const Fixed& rhs) { return *this = *this + rhs; }
    constexpr Fixed& operator-=(const Fixed& rhs) { return *this = *this - rhs; }
    constexpr Fixed& operator*=(const Fixed& rhs) { return *this = *this * rhs; }
    constexpr Fixed& operator/=(const Fixed& rhs) { return *this = *this / rhs; }

    // Three-way comparison
    constexpr auto operator<=>(const Fixed& rhs) const = default;

    // Stream output
    friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const Fixed& f) {
        return os << static_cast<double>(f);
    }
};

int main() {
    using FP = Fixed<16>;

    FP a(3);             // Implicit from int
    FP b(1.5);           // Explicit from double: Fixed<16>(1.5)
    FP c = a * b;        // 4.5
    FP d = c / FP(2);    // 2.25

    std::cout << "a=" << a << " b=" << b << " a*b=" << c << " c/2=" << d << '\n';
    // a=3 b=1.5 a*b=4.5 c/2=2.25

    double result = static_cast<double>(d);  // Explicit conversion
    std::cout << "as double: " << result << '\n';

    // Comparison operators all work via <=>
    std::cout << std::boolalpha << (a > b) << '\n';  // true
    std::cout << (FP(2.25) == d) << '\n';             // true
}
```

instruction: Implement C++ subscript operator overloading (operator[]) for a sparse matrix class that supports both read and write access via proxy objects, handles out-of-bounds gracefully, and uses move semantics for efficiency.
output:
<think>
Sparse matrix: most elements are zero, so we only store non-zero values. Typically implemented as a map from (row, col) to value.

The challenge with operator[] for a 2D structure: C++ operator[] takes one argument, so we need chaining: matrix[i][j]. This means matrix[i] returns a proxy (row proxy), and proxy[j] accesses the element.

For read access: return 0.0 (default) if element doesn't exist. For write access: only insert into storage if the written value is non-zero. This requires a proxy that defers the actual read/write.

Design:
1. SparseMatrix stores std::unordered_map<uint64_t, double> where key = row*cols + col
2. operator[](size_t row) returns RowProxy
3. RowProxy::operator[](size_t col) returns ElementProxy
4. ElementProxy has operator double() for read and operator= for write
5. ElementProxy write: if value is 0, erase from map; if non-zero, insert/update
6. Move semantics: SparseMatrix should be movable (important when storing large maps)

Const correctness: need const and non-const versions of operator[]. Const version returns a ConstRowProxy that only allows reading.
</think>

```cpp
#include <iostream>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <utility>
#include <cmath>
#include <stdexcept>

class SparseMatrix {
    size_t rows_, cols_;
    std::unordered_map<uint64_t, double> data_;
    static constexpr double ZERO_THRESHOLD = 1e-15;

    uint64_t key(size_t r, size_t c) const { return r * cols_ + c; }

public:
    SparseMatrix(size_t rows, size_t cols) : rows_(rows), cols_(cols) {}

    // Move constructor/assignment (efficient for large sparse matrices)
    SparseMatrix(SparseMatrix&& other) noexcept
        : rows_(other.rows_), cols_(other.cols_), data_(std::move(other.data_)) {
        other.rows_ = other.cols_ = 0;
    }
    SparseMatrix& operator=(SparseMatrix&& other) noexcept {
        if (this != &other) {
            rows_ = other.rows_; cols_ = other.cols_;
            data_ = std::move(other.data_);
            other.rows_ = other.cols_ = 0;
        }
        return *this;
    }
    SparseMatrix(const SparseMatrix&) = default;
    SparseMatrix& operator=(const SparseMatrix&) = default;

    // --- Element proxy for deferred read/write ---
    class ElementProxy {
        SparseMatrix& mat_;
        size_t row_, col_;
    public:
        ElementProxy(SparseMatrix& m, size_t r, size_t c) : mat_(m), row_(r), col_(c) {}

        // Read via implicit conversion
        operator double() const {
            auto it = mat_.data_.find(mat_.key(row_, col_));
            return (it != mat_.data_.end()) ? it->second : 0.0;
        }

        // Write: store non-zero, erase zero
        ElementProxy& operator=(double val) {
            uint64_t k = mat_.key(row_, col_);
            if (std::abs(val) < ZERO_THRESHOLD) {
                mat_.data_.erase(k);
            } else {
                mat_.data_[k] = val;
            }
            return *this;
        }

        // Compound assignment
        ElementProxy& operator+=(double val) { return *this = double(*this) + val; }
        ElementProxy& operator-=(double val) { return *this = double(*this) - val; }
        ElementProxy& operator*=(double val) { return *this = double(*this) * val; }
    };

    // --- Row proxy for operator[] chaining ---
    class RowProxy {
        SparseMatrix& mat_;
        size_t row_;
    public:
        RowProxy(SparseMatrix& m, size_t r) : mat_(m), row_(r) {}
        ElementProxy operator[](size_t col) {
            if (col >= mat_.cols_) throw std::out_of_range("Column index out of range");
            return ElementProxy(mat_, row_, col);
        }
    };

    // --- Const row proxy (read-only) ---
    class ConstRowProxy {
        const SparseMatrix& mat_;
        size_t row_;
    public:
        ConstRowProxy(const SparseMatrix& m, size_t r) : mat_(m), row_(r) {}
        double operator[](size_t col) const {
            if (col >= mat_.cols_) throw std::out_of_range("Column index out of range");
            auto it = mat_.data_.find(mat_.key(row_, col));
            return (it != mat_.data_.end()) ? it->second : 0.0;
        }
    };

    // --- operator[] ---
    RowProxy operator[](size_t row) {
        if (row >= rows_) throw std::out_of_range("Row index out of range");
        return RowProxy(*this, row);
    }

    ConstRowProxy operator[](size_t row) const {
        if (row >= rows_) throw std::out_of_range("Row index out of range");
        return ConstRowProxy(*this, row);
    }

    // Utility
    size_t nonzero() const { return data_.size(); }
    size_t rows() const { return rows_; }
    size_t cols() const { return cols_; }

    // Pretty print
    friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const SparseMatrix& m) {
        os << "SparseMatrix(" << m.rows_ << "x" << m.cols_
           << ", nnz=" << m.data_.size() << ")\n";
        for (const auto& [k, v] : m.data_) {
            os << "  [" << k / m.cols_ << "][" << k % m.cols_ << "] = " << v << '\n';
        }
        return os;
    }
};

int main() {
    SparseMatrix m(1000, 1000);  // 1M elements, stored sparsely

    m[0][0] = 1.0;
    m[42][99] = 3.14;
    m[999][999] = -2.5;
    m[42][99] += 1.0;  // 4.14

    // Read (returns 0.0 for unset elements)
    double val = m[500][500];  // 0.0
    std::cout << "m[42][99] = " << double(m[42][99]) << '\n';  // 4.14
    std::cout << "m[500][500] = " << val << '\n';  // 0
    std::cout << m;  // Shows 3 non-zero entries

    // Move semantics
    SparseMatrix m2 = std::move(m);  // O(1) transfer
    std::cout << "After move: " << m2.nonzero() << " entries\n";
}
```
